page: Microsoft Azure DC4ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCADCCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage, it includes a 154 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is in the top 25%, and Geekbench single-core scores sit in the middle 50%. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores, fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; read-only large block bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are average, while small block read-only bandwidth and large block write bandwidth are weak. Cryptographic capabilities are moderate, with PassMark CPU encryption and single-threaded compression/decompression benchmarks scoring in the middle 50%.

Given the hardware configuration and benchmark results, this server is appropriate for workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution over highly parallelized processing. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic text and image processing, and single-threaded application tasks. It is less appropriate for database operations, software compilation, or multi-threaded decompression, which registered weak performance in testing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and local storage provide a stable baseline for general-purpose workloads, though the resource density and multi-core performance tradeoffs should be considered for compute-heavy deployments.
description: Microsoft Azure DC4ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, a 3.525 GHz clock speed, and 16.0 GB of RAM. Storage is supported by a 154 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, placing in the top 25%, whereas multi-core performance, database operations, and software compilation fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is mixed, with average large-block read speeds but weak write speeds. This server is appropriate for workloads that rely on single-threaded performance, such as light web serving, text processing, and basic image manipulation, rather than highly parallelized database or heavy computational tasks.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and local storage, optimized for single-threaded workloads and standard application hosting.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
Includes 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Exhibits weaker multi-core scaling and database operation throughput
Appropriate for single-threaded applications, light web serving, and text processing
